name: Tibetan Spaniel
description: The Tibetan Spaniel is an ancient breed that served as a watchdog in Tibetan monasteries, often perched on walls to survey the surrounding landscape. Despite the name, they are not true spaniels and are more closely related to other Asian breeds. They are intelligent and independent, forming strong bonds with their families while remaining somewhat reserved with strangers.

name: Polish Lowland Sheepdog
description: The Polish Lowland Sheepdog, known in Poland as the PON (Polski Owczarek Nizinny), is a hardworking herding breed with a thick, shaggy double coat. They possess an excellent memory and a strong herding instinct that can extend to family members. Their confident and tenacious nature makes them best suited for experienced dog owners who can provide consistent training.

name: Wirehaired Pointing Griffon
description: The Wirehaired Pointing Griffon is often called the supreme gundog due to its versatile hunting ability on both land and water. Bred by Eduard Korthals in the 19th century, the breed combines pointing and retrieving skills with a rugged, weather-resistant coat. They are devoted family companions who thrive with active owners and ample outdoor exercise.

name: Nova Scotia Duck Tolling Retriever
description: The Nova Scotia Duck Tolling Retriever is the smallest of the retrievers, bred to lure and retrieve waterfowl through a playful tolling motion along the shoreline. Their striking red coat and fox-like appearance are matched by an intense, energetic personality that demands significant daily exercise and mental stimulation. They are affectionate and intelligent dogs that excel in dog sports and active family environments.

name: Manchester Terrier
description: The Manchester Terrier is a sleek, athletic breed developed in England for rat-catching and rabbit coursing. They are one of the oldest identified terrier breeds and combine the speed of a sighthound with the tenacity of a terrier. Alert and devoted to their families, they can be reserved with strangers and have strong prey drives that require management around small animals.

name: Spanish Water Dog
description: The Spanish Water Dog is a versatile working breed from the Iberian Peninsula, historically used for herding, hunting, and fishing tasks. Their distinctive curly or corded coat is low-shedding and requires unique care—it should never be brushed but rather allowed to cord naturally. Athletic and highly trainable, they form strong bonds with their handlers and excel in a variety of dog sports.

name: Berger Picard
description: The Berger Picard is one of France's oldest herding breeds, nearly driven to extinction after both World Wars. They have a distinctive shaggy, rough coat and an expressive face with upright ears that give them an alert, inquisitive look. Loyal and sensitive, they require early socialization and consistent training to bring out their cooperative nature.

name: American Water Spaniel
description: The American Water Spaniel is a uniquely American breed developed in the Great Lakes region for hunting waterfowl and upland birds. Compact enough to work from a small boat yet powerful enough for cold-water retrieves, they are versatile and athletic hunting companions. They are eager to please and affectionate with their families, though they can be reserved with strangers.

name: Clumber Spaniel
description: The Clumber Spaniel is the heaviest of the spaniel breeds, with a massive, rolling gait and a thoughtful, dignified demeanor. Developed as a flushing and retrieving dog for dense cover, they are tireless workers despite their slow-moving appearance. They make gentle, loyal family companions who enjoy both outdoor activities and relaxing at home with their people.

name: Bedlington Terrier
description: The Bedlington Terrier is a unique breed that resembles a lamb but has the heart of a lion, combining a gentle appearance with true terrier tenacity. Originally bred for hunting vermin in the mines of Northumberland, they are athletic, fast, and capable of surprising ferocity when challenged. Their distinctive pear-shaped head and curly, non-shedding coat require regular professional grooming.

name: Field Spaniel
description: The Field Spaniel is a rare and elegant hunting breed that nearly went extinct in the early 20th century due to extreme exaggerations in conformation. Today's Field Spaniel is a well-proportioned, active dog with a docile temperament and keen nose for field work. They are affectionate and sensitive companions who thrive with active families that can provide regular outdoor exercise.

name: Irish Red and White Setter
description: The Irish Red and White Setter is considered the older of the two Irish setter breeds, predating the solid red Irish Setter. They are athletic and energetic hunting dogs with a striking white base coat adorned with rich chestnut-red patches. Devoted and spirited, they require significant daily exercise and make excellent companions for active families with access to outdoor spaces.

name: Plott Hound
description: The Plott Hound is the official state dog of North Carolina and the only coonhound breed not descended from British foxhounds, tracing its roots instead to German boar-hunting dogs. Bred for tracking and treeing big game such as bear and boar, they are fearless, tenacious, and possess extraordinary stamina. They are loyal family dogs but retain strong hunting instincts that require experienced handling.

name: Treeing Walker Coonhound
description: The Treeing Walker Coonhound is a fast, sensible hunting dog known for its ability to track and tree game with impressive speed and efficiency. Descended from the Walker Foxhound, they have a clear, bugling bark and exceptional nose that makes them prized hunting companions across the American South. They are sociable and affectionate at home but require substantial exercise and a securely fenced yard given their strong prey drive.

name: Icelandic Sheepdog
description: The Icelandic Sheepdog is Iceland's only native dog breed, brought to the island by Norse Vikings over a thousand years ago. They were bred to herd sheep, cattle, and horses in the harsh Icelandic terrain, developing into a hardy, agile, and alert working dog. Today they are beloved for their cheerful, friendly nature and their tendency to bark, which makes them excellent watchdogs.

name: Canaan Dog
description: The Canaan Dog is one of the oldest and rarest breeds in the world, having survived as a semi-feral dog in the Middle East for thousands of years before being domesticated in the 20th century. They are Israel's national dog and were used by the Bedouin as guard and herding dogs. Highly self-reliant and alert, they are devoted to their families but wary of strangers and require experienced owners.

name: Löwchen
description: The Löwchen, meaning 'little lion dog' in German, is one of the rarest dog breeds in the world and has appeared in European paintings dating back to the 16th century. They are traditionally groomed in a lion clip with the hindquarters shaved and a full mane left on the foreparts, giving them their distinctive regal appearance. Despite their elegant look, they are robust, playful, and adaptable little dogs that thrive in various living situations.

name: Nederlandse Kooikerhondje
description: The Nederlandse Kooikerhondje is a Dutch breed that was traditionally used to lure ducks into traps called 'kooien' using its distinctive white-tipped tail. They are active, intelligent dogs with a striking orange and white coat and black-tipped ears that give them an elegant appearance. After nearly going extinct in the 1930s, careful breeding efforts restored the population and today they are cherished companion dogs in the Netherlands and beyond.

name: Otterhound
description: The Otterhound is a critically rare British breed, with fewer individual dogs than giant pandas remaining worldwide, that was originally bred to hunt otters along riverbanks. They are large, shaggy dogs with webbed feet and a dense, oily double coat that makes them exceptional swimmers. Friendly and boisterous by nature, they are affectionate family dogs that require patient training due to their independent hound temperament.

name: American Foxhound
description: The American Foxhound is one of America's oldest breeds, with roots tracing back to the 17th century and connections to George Washington's kennel. Bred for speed and endurance in fox hunting, they are lean, athletic dogs with an outstanding nose and a melodious howl. While sweet and gentle at home, they need enormous amounts of exercise and can be challenging to keep as pets without proper outlets for their energy.

name: Kuvasz
description: The Kuvasz is an ancient Hungarian flock guardian breed with a striking white double coat that allowed shepherds to distinguish it from wolves at night. They are powerful, intelligent, and fiercely protective dogs that were historically favored by Hungarian royalty. Independent and strong-willed, they require experienced owners who can provide firm, consistent leadership and early socialization from puppyhood.

name: Black and Tan Coonhound
description: The Black and Tan Coonhound is a classic American working hound developed to track and tree raccoons and other game through rugged terrain in all weather conditions. They are strong, agile dogs with an exceptionally powerful nose and a deep, resonant voice they are not shy about using. Mellow and easygoing at home, they are loyal and gentle with family but require daily exercise and a securely fenced yard.

name: Bluetick Coonhound
description: The Bluetick Coonhound is a striking American breed named for its distinctive dark blue mottled coat pattern. Bred to cold-trail and tree raccoons and other game, they possess outstanding scenting ability and relentless determination on the hunt. Devoted and affectionate with their families, they are tenacious working dogs that require experienced handling and ample daily exercise to keep them from becoming destructive.

name: Redbone Coonhound
description: The Redbone Coonhound is a swift and sure-footed American hunting breed prized for its rich red coat and ability to work in difficult terrain and water. Immortalized in the classic novel 'Where the Red Fern Grows,' they are passionate hunters with an outstanding nose and a melodious, deep voice. At home, they are affectionate, mellow dogs that are devoted to their families and get along well with children.

name: American English Coonhound
description: The American English Coonhound descends from English Foxhounds brought to colonial America and adapted over centuries to hunt raccoons and other game in demanding American terrain. They are speed machines built for long-distance running, with remarkable stamina that allows them to hunt all night. Sweet-natured and sociable at home, they need an extraordinary amount of daily exercise to be satisfied companions.

name: Harrier
description: The Harrier is a medium-sized English scent hound that resembles a smaller version of the English Foxhound, historically bred to hunt hare in packs. They are one of the rarest AKC-recognized breeds despite having a long history in England dating back to the 13th century. Outgoing and cheerful, they are excellent family dogs that need substantial daily exercise and the company of other dogs to be truly content.

name: English Foxhound
description: The English Foxhound is one of the four foxhound breeds and was specifically developed in England to hunt fox in packs alongside mounted hunters. They are powerful, well-balanced scent hounds with exceptional stamina and a friendly, sociable temperament developed through centuries of pack living. Rarely kept as house pets, they need vast amounts of exercise and the company of other dogs to thrive.

name: Sussex Spaniel
description: The Sussex Spaniel is a rare and ancient breed from Sussex, England, known for its distinctive golden-liver coat and its habit of giving tongue while hunting, unlike most spaniels. They are lower to the ground than most spaniels with a rolling gait and a solemn expression that belies their friendly and cheerful personality. Laid-back and devoted, they make excellent family companions for those who prefer a calmer, less frenetic sporting breed.

name: Finnish Lapphund
description: The Finnish Lapphund was bred by the Sami people of northern Finland to herd reindeer in Arctic conditions, developing into a hardy and agile working dog. They have a profuse double coat that protects them from extreme cold and a gentle, submissive expression that reflects their friendly and cooperative nature. Widely popular in Scandinavia, they are adaptable family dogs known for their willingness to please and exceptional loyalty.

name: Boykin Spaniel
description: The Boykin Spaniel is the official state dog of South Carolina, developed in the early 20th century as a compact turkey and waterfowl dog small enough to be transported in a canoe. They are enthusiastic, versatile hunters with a rich chocolate-brown coat and an eager-to-please attitude that makes them highly trainable. Friendly and affectionate, they make wonderful family companions with sufficient exercise.

name: Cirneco dell'Etna
description: The Cirneco dell'Etna is an ancient Sicilian sighthound that has hunted rabbits on the slopes of Mount Etna for over 2,500 years, appearing on ancient Sicilian coins. They are lean, elegant dogs with large, upright ears and a sleek coat that comes in warm chestnut and tan tones. Affectionate and gentle with their families, they retain strong hunting instincts and need secure fencing due to their prey drive and speed.

name: Pharaoh Hound
description: The Pharaoh Hound is the national dog of Malta and one of the oldest domesticated dogs in recorded history, with artifacts depicting similar dogs in ancient Egyptian art. They are graceful, athletic sighthounds with a unique trait of blushing—their nose and ears turn a deep rose color when they are excited or happy. Intelligent and friendly, they are active dogs that need daily running exercise and are known escape artists who can jump remarkably high fences.

name: Ibizan Hound
description: The Ibizan Hound is an ancient breed from the Balearic Islands of Spain, closely resembling dogs depicted in Egyptian art over 5,000 years ago. They are tall, lean, and elegant with distinctive large upright ears and a deer-like agility that allows them to jump great heights from a standstill. Athletic and affectionate with their families, they have strong prey drives and need daily vigorous exercise in securely enclosed spaces.

name: Azawakh
description: The Azawakh is a West African sighthound bred by the nomadic Tuareg people of the Sahara and Sahel regions as a guard dog, companion, and coursing hound. They are exceptionally lean and tall with a bone structure visibly beneath their thin skin, yet they are powerful and capable of incredible speed over long distances. Deeply bonded to their families but aloof with strangers, they require experienced owners who understand their independent sighthound temperament.

name: Sloughi
description: The Sloughi is a North African sighthound revered for centuries by the Berber and Arab peoples of Morocco, Algeria, Tunisia, and Libya. They are elegant, lean dogs with a smooth coat and a noble, somewhat melancholy expression that reflects their sensitive and deeply loyal nature. Though aloof with strangers, they form profound bonds with their owners and are affectionate within their family unit.

name: Bergamasco Sheepdog
description: The Bergamasco Sheepdog is an ancient Italian herding breed distinguished by its remarkable coat of three types of hair that naturally mat into flat sections called 'flocks' or 'flocs' as the dog matures. Originating in the Alps near Bergamo, this coat served as protection against both harsh weather and wolf attacks. Patient and intelligent, they are devoted family dogs that form exceptionally strong bonds with their owners.

name: Komondor
description: The Komondor is a massive Hungarian livestock guardian dog immediately recognizable by its extraordinary white corded coat that resembles a mop and can weigh up to 20 pounds on an adult dog. Brought to Hungary by nomadic Magyar tribes, they have guarded sheep and cattle for centuries, using their white coat as camouflage among the flocks. Fiercely independent and protective, they are impressive dogs that require dedicated owners willing to invest in their unique grooming needs.

name: Puli
description: The Puli is a Hungarian herding dog famous for its distinctive corded coat that naturally develops into long dreadlock-like cords as the dog matures. Agile and energetic, they were bred to herd flocks across the Hungarian plains, and they often ran across the backs of sheep when directing them. Clever and devoted, they are enthusiastic working dogs and family companions who need consistent training and regular exercise.

name: Pumi
description: The Pumi is a lively and versatile Hungarian herding dog with a distinctive curly or wavy coat and whimsical semi-erect ears that give it a unique, animated expression. Developed in the 17th and 18th centuries by crossing the Puli with German and French sheepdogs, the Pumi became a distinct breed well-suited to herding cattle and pigs in addition to sheep. Energetic and highly trainable, they excel in dog sports and need active owners who can channel their considerable drive.

name: Mudi
description: The Mudi is a rare Hungarian herding breed that developed naturally without deliberate selective breeding, making it one of the few truly naturally occurring herding breeds. Versatile and courageous, they have been used for herding, hunting wild boar, search and rescue, and even avalanche work. Lively and intelligent, they form strong bonds with their owners and thrive with active handlers who can provide them with meaningful work.

name: Schipperke
description: The Schipperke is a small Belgian breed whose name means 'little captain' in Flemish, reflecting their history as guards on canal barges between Brussels and Antwerp. They are curious, mischievous, and energetic little dogs with a fox-like face and a distinctive thick ruff around the neck. Despite their small size, they are bold and confident dogs that need mental stimulation and regular exercise to prevent them from inventing their own entertainment.

name: Swedish Vallhund
description: The Swedish Vallhund is an ancient Viking dog breed that has herded cattle in Scandinavia for over a thousand years, with a body shape similar to a Welsh Corgi but developed entirely independently. Compact and muscular with a spitz-type look and a variable tail length that may be naturally bobbed, they are energetic and versatile working dogs. Alert, playful, and eager to please, they excel in dog sports and make devoted family companions for active households.

name: Norwegian Buhund
description: The Norwegian Buhund is a versatile spitz breed from Norway that served as a herding dog, guard dog, and hunting companion for Norwegian farmers for centuries. They are athletic and energetic dogs with a tightly curled tail and a wedge-shaped head typical of Nordic breeds. Enthusiastic and affectionate, they form strong bonds with their families and require daily exercise and mental stimulation to be satisfied companions.

name: Lagotto Romagnolo
description: The Lagotto Romagnolo is an Italian breed traditionally used as a water retriever in the marshes of Romagna and is now world-famous as a truffle-hunting dog. They are sturdy, medium-sized dogs with a dense, woolly, water-resistant coat and an exceptional nose that makes them the only breed specifically certified for truffle hunting. Affectionate and devoted, they form strong bonds with their families and excel in nose work and detection activities.

name: Bracco Italiano
description: The Bracco Italiano is one of Europe's oldest pointing breeds, depicted in Italian art dating back to the 5th century BC and developed to its current form by Italian nobility during the Renaissance. They are large, athletic gundogs with a distinctive long, pendulous muzzle and velvety, drop ears that give them a noble, serious expression. Gentle and people-oriented, they are devoted family dogs that need substantial daily exercise to be content.

name: Stabyhoun
description: The Stabyhoun is one of the rarest dog breeds in the world, with fewer than 7,000 individuals existing globally, native to the Frisian forest region of the Netherlands. They are gentle, versatile hunting dogs historically used for pointing, retrieving, and even as a mole-catching dog on farms. Affectionate and patient, they are excellent family dogs known for their quiet, willing nature and strong bonds with their families.

name: Drentsche Patrijshond
description: The Drentsche Patrijshond, or Dutch Partridge Dog, is a versatile gundog from the Dutch province of Drenthe that has been used for pointing, flushing, and retrieving game for over 400 years. They are gentle, loyal dogs known for their strong desire to please and their ability to work closely with their handler rather than roaming independently. An exceptional family dog as well as a capable hunter, they are devoted and affectionate companions.

name: Grand Basset Griffon Vendéen
description: The Grand Basset Griffon Vendéen is a scent hound from the Vendée region of France, known for its joyful, outgoing character and shaggy rough coat that gives it an endearingly unkempt appearance. They are pack hunters developed to pursue hare and rabbit over rough terrain, combining stamina with a nose of extraordinary sensitivity. Though independent and single-minded on the trail, they are cheerful and affectionate companions at home.

name: Petit Basset Griffon Vendéen
description: The Petit Basset Griffon Vendéen, affectionately known as the PBGV, is a small French scent hound with a big personality and a distinctive rough, tousled coat that gives it a happy, carefree look. Though compact, they are robust and energetic hunters bred to track rabbit through dense underbrush in the Vendée region. Extroverted and lively, they are delightful family companions known for their happy disposition and seemingly permanent wagging tail.

name: Glen of Imaal Terrier
description: The Glen of Imaal Terrier is a rare Irish breed from the remote Glen of Imaal in County Wicklow, developed as a working dog to hunt fox and badger and to turn a cooking spit via a treadmill wheel. Despite their small stature, they are sturdy and muscular terriers with a distinctly bowed front and a spirited, courageous temperament. More laid-back than many terrier breeds, they are devoted and affectionate family dogs that retain the typical terrier stubbornness.
